content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the sum of the digits present in an alphanumeric string.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase/uppercase English letters, along with digits 0 to 9. (There shall be no space in the string)


Output Format

For each test case, print the sum of digits in each string.
constraints: Strings contain only printable ASCII characters; no spaces.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty alphanumeric string (letters, digits and possible symbols) without spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of all decimal digits (0‑9) that appear in the given string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Sum of digits 1 through 9 plus 0 equals 45.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the string character by character. Whenever a character lies between '0' and '9' we add its numeric value (ch-'0') to a running total. After processing the whole string we output the total. The scan is linear, uses O(1) extra memory and works for any printable ASCII input.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) – each character is examined once.
